steps.title: Identify the causal chain the passage sets up
steps.content: The passage establishes two things: (1) red is associated with power and causes caution/hesitation in those who encounter it, and (2) Sheffield United wears mainly red at home. The phrase “these findings, if applicable in the context of athletic competitions, could suggest” asks us to apply the red-effect to opponents playing at Sheffield United’s stadium. The blank must describe what opponents may have experienced as a result of seeing Sheffield United’s red uniforms.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘been influenced by the color of Sheffield United's uniforms.’: directly applies the research — red causes caution and hesitation, so opponents would have been influenced by the red uniforms, explaining Sheffield United’s home success. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘played more aggressively than they would have if their own uniforms were red.’: shifts focus to the opponents’ own uniform color, which the passage never discusses as a factor. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘been unaffected by the red clothing worn by fans in the stadium.’: contradicts the passage’s implication that red does have an effect on opponents; it also misidentifies the source of red as fan clothing rather than Sheffield United’s uniforms. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘been aware that Sheffield United's uniform color affected their own performance.’: introduces conscious awareness, but the passage describes an automatic psychological response — not deliberate recognition of the color’s influence. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
